title: Acajuba occidentalis
taxonomy: Acajuba occidentalis belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legume or pea family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its compound leaves and fruit, which are typical of the Fabaceae family. Acajuba occidentalis is also classified as a species within the genus Acajuba. The genus Acajuba is part of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, which includes other plants with similar characteristics. Acajuba occidentalis is native to South America.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and the management of fever.
